name: none
title: None
description: No associated symptom.

name: salivation
title: Salivation
description: Drooling and excessive oral secretions.

name: lacrimation
title: Lacrimation
description: Uncontrolled, profuse tearing.

name: urination
title: Urination
description: Involuntary or frequent urination.

name: defecation
title: Defecation
description: Sudden and severe diarrhea or bowel incontinence.

name: emesis
title: Emesis
description: Persistent nausea and vomiting

name: hyperactive
title: Hyperactive
description: Abnormally high energy, restlessness, or excessive physical and mental activity, fidgeting, constant moving.

name: hypoactive
title: Hypoactive
description: Less active or underactive compared to the normal range. Functions, cognitive states, or physiological responses  are slower, quieter, or diminished in frequency or intensity.

name: hyperactiveBowels
title: Hyperactive Bowel Sounds
description: Abdominal cramping and hyperactive bowel sounds

name: hypoactiveBowels
title: Hypoactive Bowel Sounds
description: a decrease in the loudness, tone, or regularity of the noises made by the intestines. In a medical context, normal digestion produces 5 to 30 gurgling or rumbling sounds per minute; hypoactive sounds are quieter or occur at a rate of less than 5 per minute

name: bronchospasm
title: Bronchospasm
description: spasmodic contraction of bronchial muscles that obstructs breathing

name: bronchorrhea
title: Bronchorrhea
description: excessive production of watery, thin mucus (sputum) from the lungs and airways. It is clinically defined as producing more than 100 mL of sputum in a 24-hour period, though severe cases can exceed 1000 mL per day.

name: bradycardia
title: Bradycardia
description: resting heart rate of less than 60 bpm, for most individuals

name: anhidrosis
title: Anhidrosis And Dry Mucosa
description: Complete lack of sweating, causing dry armpits, as well as a bone-dry mouth and throat

name: diaphoresis
title: Diaphoresis
description: Sweating profusely

name: piloerection
title: Piloerection
description: Goosebumps, tiny bumps around the hairs

name: flushing
title: Flushed
description: Cutaneous vasodilation often appears as flushed, red, and warm skin

name: mydriasis
title: Dilated Pupils
description: Severely dilated pupils that do not react to light, causing blurry vision and sensitivity to light (photophobia)

name: miosis
title: Pin point Pupils
description: pupils are abnormally constricted, pin point

name: delirium
title: Delirium
description: Altered mental status, neurologic and psychiatric effects such as agitated delirium, acute confusion

name: hallucination
title: Hallucination
description: Terrifying visual or auditory hallucinations, and incoherent speech. Patients may exhibit 'phantom behaviors,' such as aimlessly picking at clothing or the air

name: hyperthermia
title: Elevated Temperature
description: a medical condition where the body's core temperature rises significantly above normal (usually above 38° C or 100.4° F) because the body's natural heat-regulation system is overwhelmed or fails. It differs from a fever, as the body’s internal temperature setting is not actively raised by the brain

name: hypothermia
title: Hypothermia
description: a dangerously low core body temperature. It is defined as any temperature dropping below 95° F (35° C).

name: urinaryRetention
title: Urinary Retention
description: inability to empty the bladder

name: tachycardia
title: Elevated Heart Rate
description: heart rate that is faster than normal. It is defined as a resting heart rate of more than 100 beats per minute

name: unconscious
title: Unconscious
description: is a state of complete or near-complete lack of awareness of self and the environment, where a person is entirely unresponsive to external stimuli. Unlike sleep, an unconscious person cannot be easily woken or roused by shaking or loud noises.

name: anuria
title: Anuria
description: The suppression or failure of the kidneys to produce urine. It is typically defined as passing less than 100 mL of urine in a 24-hour period.

name: oliguria
title: Oliguria
description: Abnormally low volume of urine output. It is usually defined as producing less than 400 mL of urine in a 24-hour period.

name: personalSafety
title: Personal Safety Risk
description: Exhibiting behaviour that is dangerous to people around them.

name: publicSafety
title: Public Safety Risk
description: Exhibiting behaviour that is dangerous to themselves, attempting to harm or kill themselves.

name: fasciculation
title: Involuntary muscle twitching
description: Involuntary muscle twitching and muscle weakness

name: disappeared
title: Disappearance
description: The patient has left home without knowledge of where they are going.

name: suicideNote
title: Suicide Note
description: Left a note describing intention to disappear or commit suicide.

name: accessToMeans
title: Access To Means
description: Has access to the ways and means to kill themselves

name: attempted
title: Attempted
description: Has attempted suicide at one or more points in the past.

name: attempting
title: Attempting
description: Is currently on the way to, or in the process of, committing suicide.

name: discovered
title: Discovered
description: THe person has recently been discovered attempting suicide.

name: selfHarmedWithIntention
title: Self Harmed With Intention
description: The person had recently hurt themselves with the intention of killing themselves.

name: selfHarmingWithIntention
title: Self Harming With Intention
description: Is currently in the process of hurting or physically abusing themselves with the intent of killing themselves.

name: researched
title: Researched
description: Person has recently researched or is researching ways to kill themselves.

name: riskyBehaviour
title: Risky Behaviour
description: The person has recently been taking foolish risks such as driving extremely fast.

name: mentioned
title: Talks About Suicide
description: The person has recently been discussing suicide or the methods of committing it.

name: feeling
title: Feeling Sad
description: The person has been depressed or deeply sad.

name: anhedonia
title: Anhedonia
description: Sudden loss of interest in daily activities

name: avolition
title: Avolition
description: Significant decline in self-care and personal hygiene
